problem_description: A canonical paper page has a mechanism section that is too shallow for a new researcher to use in implementation or ablation planning. The user supplies a refinement note explaining what mechanism distinction should be preserved.
expected_result: A refinement proposal is created against the canonical paper page, affected sections include mechanism-oriented sections, source facts are not rewritten directly, and publish creates a version snapshot plus an Evolution Notes entry.
acceptable_paths: propose-refine by exact title or alias
must_not_do: Do not overwrite the canonical page without snapshot
Do not claim user text is paper source fact
notes: Outcome properties matter more than exact wording.

problem_description: The user says a source-grounded statement in the paper page is wrong. This may be true, but the correction must be checked against source pages/PDF before changing Source Facts.
expected_result: The refinement is classified as source_fact_correction, source_recheck_required is true, lint fails if it is false, and publish does not rewrite source-grounded sections from user text alone.
acceptable_paths: automatic source re-check requirement from note wording
must_not_do: Do not accept source fact correction without source_recheck_required
Do not promote user correction into Evidence Map as paper evidence

problem_description: A published User Insights entry should influence page interpretation or retrieval hooks without becoming source fact.
expected_result: propose-refine can reference from_insight, provenance_inputs includes the insight id, publish records the integration in Evolution Notes, and retrieval marks user insight separately if matched.
acceptable_paths: manual note that references the insight
must_not_do: Do not move user insight into Source Facts
Do not remove the original User Insights entry

problem_description: The user wants a page to be found for a new research intent that is not currently covered by retrieval metadata.
expected_result: The refinement records retrieval impact, affected sections include When To Retrieve This Paper or Evolution Notes, and future retrieval exposes the updated latest revision context.
must_not_do: Do not add opaque keyword stuffing
Do not index old snapshots in normal retrieval

problem_description: A synthesis comparison page should evolve after new inputs change the interpretation or decision boundary.
expected_result: The refinement targets a synthesis page, creates a synthesis snapshot, appends Evolution Notes, and leaves Source Facts / Wiki Synthesis / User Ideas boundaries reviewable.
must_not_do: Do not create a disconnected new answer instead of updating canonical synthesis

problem_description: A claim or evidence section is stale or superseded and should warn future retrieval users.
expected_result: Published refinement sets evolution_state or evolution_markers such as stale/superseded/needs_source_recheck, and retrieval context shows an evolution warning.
must_not_do: Do not silently hide stale status
Do not treat stale section as settled evidence
evaluation_rubric: warning is visible in retrieval packet
log records why the page evolved

problem_description: A target query matches multiple canonical pages.
expected_result: propose-refine writes a blocked disambiguation artifact and exits nonzero; no canonical page is mutated.
must_not_do: Do not pick a page arbitrarily

problem_description: A refinement proposal was generated, then the target canonical page changed before publish.
expected_result: refinement-lint detects target_revision_before mismatch and blocks publish until proposal is regenerated or rebased.
must_not_do: Do not apply old proposal over changed target
Do not overwrite latest page silently

problem_description: Old snapshots exist after refinement but should not pollute normal retrieval.
expected_result: Normal meridian wiki retrieve scans canonical papers/syntheses only; .versions pages are excluded unless a future explicit history mode is added.
must_not_do: Do not rank snapshot as current paper
Do not expose snapshot as canonical path
